---
title: "City2Graph · Post-process a graph"
---

::: {.lead}
Clean up and subset any graph object: remove isolated components, filter by
distance from a center, clip to a polygon, and build isochrones.
:::

This walkthrough mirrors the
[Graph Tools panel](../../panels/city2graph/graph-tools.qmd).

## Before you begin

Have any graph object active — a street network, a proximity graph, or a
morphological graph.

## 1. Choose an action

In **Graph Tools**, set the **Action**: **Remove Isolated**, **Filter**, **Clip**
or **Isochrone**.

![The Graph Tools action selector.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/07-action.png)

## 2. Remove isolated components

With **Remove Isolated**, press **Apply** to keep only the largest connected
component, discarding stray fragments.

![Keeping only the largest connected component.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/07-remove-isolated.png)

## 3. Filter by distance

With **Filter**, set the **Center** object and a **Threshold** distance, then
**Apply** to keep nodes within that distance.

![Filtering the graph by distance from a center.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/07-filter.png)

## 4. Clip to a polygon

With **Clip**, set the clip mesh and **Apply** to keep only the part of the graph
inside the polygon.

![Clipping the graph to a polygon.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/07-clip.png)

## 5. Build an isochrone

With **Isochrone**, set the **Isochrone Center**, a **Threshold** and the
**Weight Attr** (e.g. `travel_time`), then **Apply** to extract the reachable
sub-network.

![An isochrone sub-network from a center node.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/07-isochrone.png)

## Next steps

Continue with [Export for GeoAI](export.qmd) to take a graph into a GNN
framework.
